mnemonics.title: Karatsuba 3-Step Magic
mnemonics.content: Remember Karatsuba: 'A1 B1 + A0 B0 + (A1+A0)(B1+B0) - A1B1 - A0B0' simplifies to 3 multiplications instead of 4 using: z₂ (high×high), z₀ (low×low), z₁ = (sum of As)×(sum of Bs) - z₂ - z₀.

mnemonics.title: Strassen's 7 Matrix Recipe
mnemonics.content: M1: A×(F-H), M2: (A+B)×H, M3: (C+D)×E, M4: D×(G-E), M5: (A+D)×(E+H), M6: (B-D)×(G+H), M7: (A-C)×(E+F) - 'A B C D E F G H' are the 8 quadrants.

mnemonics.title: Karatsuba vs Strassen Exponents
mnemonics.content: Karatsuba: 'Kar-3-suba' → 3 multiplications → exponent log₂3 ≈ 1.585. Strassen: 'Strap-7-sen' → 7 multiplications → exponent log₂7 ≈ 2.807.

acronyms.usage: Used in O(n log n) integer multiplication for very large numbers (typically >10,000 digits)
Generalization of Karatsuba splitting into k parts; Karatsuba is Toom-Cook with k=3

visualTips.visualization: Think of splitting a 4-digit number 1234 as: a₁=12 (left half), a₀=34 (right half), with base 10^(n/2)=10^2=100. So 1234 = 12×100 + 34.
Draw a 2×2 block matrix: top-left is A₁₁ (E), top-right is A₁₂ (F), bottom-left is A₂₁ (G), bottom-right is A₂₂ (H). Results C have same quadrant structure.
C = [M4+M5, M1+M2; M3+M6, M1+M3+M5-M7] - Notice C₁₁ uses M4+M5, C₁₂ uses M1+M2, C₂₁ uses M3+M6, C₂₂ uses M1+M3+M5-M7.

analogies.analogy: Like calculating (100+x)(100+y) = 10000 + 100(x+y) + xy by knowing x+y and xy, instead of calculating 100×100, 100×y, x×100, x×y separately.
Like finding 8 products by clever rearrangement to get only 7, similar to finding a shortcut in mental math instead of doing all individual multiplications.
Like multiplying two 2×2 block matrices by treating each block as a single number, but each 'number' is itself an n/2 × n/2 matrix.
